text:
| process_explanation__dra_hearing__child_arrangements | child_arrangements | Common mistakes at the DRA — e.g. not reading the Section 7 report carefully, responding to CAFCASS's recommendation by arguing parental rights rather than the child's welfare, missing the opportunity for settlement, not understanding how to challenge factual errors in the Section 7 report |
| legal_principle__welfare_principle__child_arrangements | child_arrangements | What unrepresented people most commonly get wrong about the welfare principle — e.g. believing the court automatically favours the primary carer, misunderstanding that a child's expressed wishes are not determinative, not understanding that conduct toward the other parent is only relevant insofar as it affects the child |
| legal_principle__section_25_factors__financial_remedy | financial_remedy | What unrepresented people most commonly get wrong about the section 25 factors — e.g. assuming 50/50 is the default, not understanding that conduct rarely affects the outcome, overlooking pension, conflating earning capacity with current earnings |
| legal_principle__paramountcy_vs_balance__child_arrangements | child_arrangements | What unrepresented people most commonly get wrong about parental rights vs the welfare principle — e.g. believing the court will correct unfairness between adults, not understanding that parental responsibility is not removed by a child arrangements order, believing primary carer status automatically prevails |
| legal_principle__duty_of_full_and_frank_disclosure__financial_remedy | financial_remedy | What unrepresented people most commonly get wrong about the disclosure duty — e.g. believing assets held in another's name need not be disclosed, not knowing the duty continues after Form E, underestimating the consequences of non-disclosure, not knowing a consent order can be set aside for pre-agreement non-disclosure |
| case_law_summary__white_v_white__financial_remedy | financial_remedy | What unrepresented people most commonly misunderstand about the yardstick of equality — e.g. whether they believe it guarantees 50/50, or whether they understand it as a check rather than a starting point |
